events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: flashing not lapped/sealed in a residential masonry job, rough-complete stage. TMS 402 and BIA tech notes require flashing to be lapped and sealed properly to direct water out. Flashing not lapped/sealed is non-compliant and defective. Decision: fail. Risk: high (water intrusion potential). Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 70. Confidence high.

"findings":["Flashing is not lapped and sealed at joints, allowing water entry behind the masonry veneer","Missing/incorrect end dams and lap continuity at flashing terminations","Weep path may be compromised if flashing does not drain to weeps properly"],
"actions":["Re-lap flashing a minimum of 2 inches and seal laps with compatible sealant per manufacturer instructions","Install/verify end dams at openings and terminations","Confirm weeps are clear and located at lowest course above flashing","Re-inspect before closing in the wall"],
"rationale":"TMS 402 and BIA technical notes require through-wall flashing to be continuous, properly lapped, sealed, and drained to weeps. Flashing that is not lapped/sealed will allow water into the wall assembly and does not meet prescriptive masonry veneer requirements.",
"references":["TMS 402 - Flashing and weep requirements for masonry veneer","BIA Technical Notes on Flashing and Weep Holes"]
